title: linum hirsutum subsp. lanuginosum
intro: Linum hirsutum subsp. lanuginosum, also known as woolly-flowered flax or woolly blue flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum hirsutum subsp. lanuginosum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, this plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
